domain: Maori taonga pūoro wind instruments (kōauau, nguru, putorino, putatara; breath-driven; designed for microtones outside Western semitone grid; pitch micro-control via finger-pressure + breath)
specific_mechanism: Hybrid pitch-quantization codec map for TTS/speech LLM: in default speech-frequency range [F_low, F_high] apply standard semitone-bin quantization (100-cent bins); in emotional/expressive sub-regions [F_emo_lo, F_emo_hi] (e.g., 200-400 Hz prosody-carry band) apply DENSE 25-cent or 50-cent micro-bin quantization. Codebook is non-uniformly allocated: standard regions get K bins, emotional region gets K_dense bins (4-5x density).
llm_application: TAONGA-MICRO-BAND: a non-uniform pitch-quantization codec for speech LLMs. Pitch token codebook is allocated NON-UNIFORMLY across the frequency axis: (a) default-speech-frequency band [F_low, F_high] uses standard semitone (100-cent) bins; (b) prosody-emotional-locus band [F_emo_lo, F_emo_hi] uses dense 25-cent or 50-cent micro-bins (4-5× density of default); (c) high-frequency tail uses coarser ~200-cent bins. Total codebook size unchanged but local resolution is concentrated where expressive resolution matters. Differs from Spark-TTS 2503.01710 (uniform fine-grained pitch tokens via attribute tokenizer), SAC 2510.16841 (dual-stream semantic-acoustic, uniform within each stream), VoxCPM (tokenizer-free, no quantization map), CosyVoice 2 (FSQ uniform quantization). Differs from R383 YORUBA-DUNDUN (3-bucket H/M/L tonal-language) by NON-UNIFORM ACROSS-AXIS density allocation tied to emotional-prosody locus, not categorical-tonal target.
novelty_claim: Spark-TTS + SAC + VoxCPM + CosyVoice 2 + Discrete Multimodal Tokenization Survey 2507.22920 cover fine-grained pitch quantization for speech-LLM. TAONGA-MICRO-BAND adds NON-UNIFORM emotional-locus dense-bin allocation. Recombination — non-uniform quantization is a standard signal-processing technique (μ-law, A-law), recombination with TTS codec design.
